steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says labeling the novel ‘autobiographical’ may lead readers to believe Fitzgerald ‘merely fictionalized true events’ — and in a field where ‘creativity and inventiveness are prized,’ that perception can suggest something negative. The blank must complete that negative suggestion logically.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘This Side of Paradise is less of an imaginative achievement than it actually is’: If readers think Fitzgerald only transcribed real life, they'd rate the novel's creative achievement lower than it deserves — exactly the downgrade ‘merely’ implies in a creativity-prizing field. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Fitzgerald should not have claimed that This Side of Paradise is based on real events’: The passage is about readers' perceptions, not a moral judgment on Fitzgerald's conduct. Nothing in the passage criticizes Fitzgerald's claims. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘critics disagree about whether This Side of Paradise shows greater originality than works without autobiographical elements’: The passage mentions no critical disagreement at all. This is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘the real-world counterparts of other characters in This Side of Paradise are hard to identify’: Character sourcing is off-topic; the passage is about perceived artistic achievement, not identifying real-world models. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
